analysis.consistency: The spell data appears internally consistent with standard D&D 5e spell formatting. Components, casting time, range, and duration are clearly defined.
analysis.quality: High quality. The description is clear and references standard game mechanics ('advantage', 'invisible', 'Dexterity saving throw'). The effect (outlining in light) is easy to visualize and apply.

mechanics.save_or_succeed: Dexterity saving throw (for initial area creatures)
mechanics.primary_effect: Outlines objects/creatures in colored light within a 20-foot cube.
mechanical_benefits: Advantage on attack rolls against affected creatures/objects (if visible).
mechanics.mechanics_notes: The requirement for attackers to 'see' the target mitigates the advantage if heavy fog or similar obscurement is present, which is a reasonable interaction for a light-based spell.

analysis.archivist_notes: A solid Level 1 battlefield control/support spell. The area of effect (20-foot cube) is substantial for its level. The mechanic of negating invisibility is highly valuable.
